text.title: Provider Deprecation Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every provider-deprecation runbook must enforce.

rule.statement: Every step names an actor (role + system). 'Someone notifies' rejected.
Every side-effect step ships rollback OR explicit STOP branch.
rule.rationale: Mid-procedure stuck states cause incident escalation.

rule.statement: Every phase carries a wall-clock budget (minutes). Overrun triggers escalation.
rule.rationale: Window-blowing runs go undetected until the second alarm.

rule.statement: Runbook ships a run-record schema (start_ts, end_ts, branches, exceptions, links). Committed after execution.
rule.rationale: Without records the runbook cannot improve.

rule.statement: Header names a single owner + on-call escalation path.
Deprecation calendar known AND owner + on-call named → run the 5-step procedure.
Multi-provider portability already locked OR no deprecation pending → skip; rely on portability spec rollback.
